So I want to give you some tips here that really were outside the main core trading here. What I'm referring to specifically is this customization of your patterns to directly suit the industry you're in. In other words, if you are selling automobiles, you're not going to look at a customer and say, so let me ask you a question. Does the car make sense to you? Do you like the car? Let me be like, what the hell is wrong with you? You just can't do it, right? So I sent you, I'd help you customize your patterns in the back end tier to make it easy. Let me explain how we're going to do that. Two-step process. Step number one, I'm going to give you some pointers right now that are pretty, at least to me, seem self-evident, right? But then also, I'm going to ask you to simply write in if you're struggling. Whatever your industry is, tell me what your industry is. Tell me the approach. It's got to be under one minute or less, and I'm going to answer that in our weekly intensives as many as come in. I want to make sure every single person can easily use these, essentially hands you on a silver platter for all three tens. It's easy to do. Let's start with rule number one here. What are you really doing when you loop? Just think about it. What's really going on? What's something just leaning back, no fancy words. What are you really doing, right? You know, what's really happening is you're just looking at the prospect, you know, I may have to demand one more thing. Like, you know, let me ask you a question. Do you like what I'm offering you? That's what you know. Do you like this? Does it make sense? What do you think? Is this good or not? Do you see what I see? Just the psychology. That's what's going on. In other words, you make this presentation, you know, you go through this whole process, but you know, all things be equal, what do you think? What do you think of the product? How does it grab you? Do you like the car? And what is it? Does it tick all the boxes? What do you think? Do you love it? Tell me. That's just what you're trying to accomplish. All right? It's simply a must know. Like, you know, how certain are you that my product meets your needs? Are you real? Wow, I know that. Love it. It's great. It's beautiful. It's awesome. So what do you think? Like, what do you think? It's simple. Like, just my point is this pattern can be as simple as, tell me, what do you think? What do you think? What do you think? The lens has a grab you. What do you think? It's as simple as that. Now, obviously you can usually do better than that. But even if you just sit, so tell me, what do you think? And guys, that's pretty good. He's at a five or six. So the point is in some cases when you're in financial services, offering coaching programs, anything that's really more of an idea, a service. Let me ask you a question. Does the program make sense to you? Do you like the program? How does it grab you? There's no mystery here. There's no like, you know, I'm going to get the whole grand card don't issue because there's no tricks. But the point is, is there's not like some magic, you know, fancy pattern that's going to do that much better than you saying, what do you think? Now I give you these perfectly constructed patterns that sound great because whatever you want to, whatever you can, you want to sound awesome, right? But don't think for a second that at the end of the day, you could just say, so tell me, what do you think has a grab you? Do you like to see what do you think about that? Does this fit your needs? All you're looking to do is find out where they are on the certainty scale for the first hand. That's it. So don't make yourself nuts with like, you don't have to say, well, sir, I hear what you're saying, but let me ask you a question. Does the program, I hear what you're saying, does the car make sense to you? I hear it. Does the painting make sense to you? They're like, what the hell are you talking about? So don't worry about this. Be logical, be smart. And most important, remember, it's just not sounding natural, but in a way that is very directed. What do you think? What are you trying to accomplish? I want to know where he or she stands on the certainty scale for the first 10. That's it. Once I know that, I can then have a basis to start building certainty. And then my second pattern now, right, is going to be that forest gum pattern. So imagine if you set the saw and let me show, if I've been selling you flowers for the last three or four years, they're like, huh? And you'd loving my flowers on a consistent basis. You probably would be saying, let me think about the bouquet Jordan. You'd be saying, pick me up a whole bouquet. It doesn't work. Like for many things, but all you're saying here, what's the real gist? If you knew me, if you know how much I can, what a great job I did for my people, you probably wouldn't be saying, let me think about it. You'd be saying, let's get started. Just do it. Tell me what to do. I mean, so there's so many ways to go over getting to that great truth, which is, you know what, if you want someone to trust them, tell them why they should trust you. My name's Forrest Forrest. Come tell who my name is Jordan Belfort. I'm a senior vice president of XYZ company and I pride myself on XYZ or DEF or ABG, whatever you tell them who you are. Don't get so locked down in the officialness or the automation of these patterns. What needs to happen here, what you're doing is remember what this is really about. In the back half of the sale, we had this problem. It's a challenge where what started out as this healthy collaboration tends to very often turn into confrontation as they're giving you their so-called objection, whatever it might be. And then you have to overcome that objection. That's not really an effective way of closing. We don't overcome objections. Whenever possible, we want to answer objections before they ever come up by seeding them into our presentations. So we find things out during the intelligence gathering phase, right? And then you seed things into your presentation that you know will really help quell pain points. That's obvious. We went through that already. But then also, the next level up from that is simply looping back around into presentation mode. So we use this objection as an opportunity to essentially loop back. That's what we're doing. Loop back into presentation mode. Now you start that loop. There's an infinite number of ways. Sometimes you'll actually be consolidating all of them into one pattern, meaning you won't be saying, does that make sense? Let's say you say, you found me so far great. Now if I'd been your broker, sometimes it might just be, and also by the way, you don't really know me. Let me just tell you a bit more about myself. You might not have to ask that, have I been your broker or if you know me. You just play both sides. It depends on the industry that you're in. You understand this? I can't stress this enough. Flexibility is the key. Now again, I'm asking you so no one could ever say you told me you had it to me in a store. If you don't understand this, write it in. I demand or else don't complain about it, right? Because I will solve your problem, but I want to do it publicly for everybody because there are many permutations here. I'll get 10, 15 questions, we'll resolve them all, and you'll be flying free. You'll be closing anyone who's closeable. All right? So the reason why I just divided this into two chunks, looping part one, looping part two, is I want you to take stock in this first loop. This first loop is everything. This first loop sets the stage for all loops that come after. In other words, the tactic of taking an objection, then deflecting that objection, right? And then using it as an opportunity to build more certainty for each of the three 10s and transitioning into the close. That's what you're doing. Now, after that first loop, you're not going to be deflecting anymore. You'll be answering objections and on. You'll get to that now in the part two of this module, which you'll have in a few days. I don't want you to really focus in on this one. Don't jump ahead here, please. All right? And from there, there are many ways to skin a cat. In other words, sometimes you'll be talking about things all at once, meaning you'll go from your first, second, third. You'll almost be melding these patterns together when that makes the most sense. Other times you'll be following a very distinct roadmap, you know, boom, boom, boom. First 10, second 10, third 10, just the way I do it in the training. But remember, the overriding factor for this whole thing, the big tamale here, is that if at any moment in time you turn this into a wooden exercise where it sounds unnatural or it's forced, it ain't going to work. That's the opposite of what straight line scripting over like I'm starting to call this dynamic presenting is all about. It's about learning how to use these manufactured patterns, these blocks of perfectly constructed information and move them around the board, little chess pieces, so they make perfect sense. You know, the beginning, you know, the end, you hook them together. And by doing that, you sound perfectly brilliant, sincere on point, an expert, and you're not reading from a script. That's when looping truly is being used, the way looping should be used. Otherwise, yeah, you'll get some, you know, mileage out of it because, you know, you just raise certainty, yeah, that works. But no, that's not what this is about, my friend. When you really get this, you're going to see a blow the doors off increase in your closing rate.
